title: Stable transfection protocol of unicellular relative to animals Corallochytrium limacisporum
steps: Plasmid used contains PAC gene (puromycin resistance), fused to mcherry protein under endogenous promoter (actin). See: https://www.protocols.io/view/transient-transfection-of-unicellular-relative-to-hmwb47e
Centrifuge at 1500 g for 5 minutes Increase final volume by 1:2 dilution
Transfer into appropiate flask whilst increasing final volume of fresh medium. Culture should increase number of transformants with time and cells that didn't integrate the plasmid should die.

Grow single colonies separately, label accordingly. In principle this would mean that the culture is stably transformed -- Need to perform other assays to verify plasmid integration, this would include: -cycles of freezing/thawing -southern blot -qPCR -flow cytometry
creator: Maria Rubio-Brotons
